text.title: fco-spot-instances Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for fco-spot-instances. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Any workload running on Spot MUST implement: (1) graceful shutdown handler for the 2-minute interruption notice, (2) application checkpointing for jobs longer than 10 minutes, (3) automatic fallback to on-demand if Spot is unavailable for more than 10 minutes, and (4) instance type diversification across at least 3 instance families and 2 availability zones.
rule.rationale: A single failure mode breaks all four requirements: if any one is missing, interruptions cause data loss, job restarts, or service unavailability that exceeds the cost savings. Diversification prevents the "no capacity" failure where a single instance type is fully claimed by on-demand demand in all AZs simultaneously.

rule.statement: Configure Spot Fleet or ASG mixed instances policy with at least 3-4 instance types of equivalent vCPU and memory across 2+ availability zones. Never request a single Spot instance type — the combination of high demand + single type creates frequent unavailability.
rule.rationale: A single instance type (e.g., c5.4xlarge) may have 100% interruption frequency in a region during peak demand. Diversifying across c5.4xlarge, c5a.4xlarge, c6i.4xlarge, and m5.4xlarge means at least one variant will have available capacity.
